steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure on each side of the blank
steps.content: Left side: “she's best known” — an independent clause. Right side: “especially since her 2020 novel Mexican Gothic reached the New York Times Best Sellers list” — a dependent phrase starting with a subordinating term.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: An em-dash introduces elaborating information after an independent clause, signaling that what follows explains or adds context to the main claim. The other options either lack the needed clarity (no punctuation), use inappropriate marks for dependent phrases (a colon functions differently), or misidentify what follows as an independent clause (a semicolon only joins independent clauses). That matches choice C — C is correct.
